content_en: Indeed the heart testifies that the Prophet -sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallamnever said even one of the letters from this statement. May Allaah disfigure the one who fabricated this.' S a l a f i P u b l i c a t i o n s . c o m Page 10 Raml The Shariah' Reasoning for the Brisk Walk During Tawwaaf No. 2573 - On the authority of Ibn Abbas –Rahiallaahu anhu- 'The Quraish said: 'Indeed Muhammad and his Companions have been weakened by the fever of Yathrib.' So in the year the Messenger of Allaah - sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallam – performed Umrah he said to his Companions: 'Walk briskly around the Ka'bah so the Mushrikeen can see your strength.' So when they walked briskly the Quraish said: 'It has not weakened them.' sahih Silsilah-Saheehah Shaykh Albaani said: 'This chain is sahih according to the conditions of Imam Muslim. Bukhari narrates it without a complete chain in his 'sahih' (5/86) on the authority of Hamad bin Salamah in a narration similar to this, as does Hamad bin Zayd in a narration on the authority of Ayoob whose wording is as follows: 'The Prophet -sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallamcommanded them to walk briskly for three circuits and walk calmly between the two corners so the Mushrikeen could see their strength and patience.' So the Mushrikeen said: 'These are those whom you claimed had been weakened by the fever. They are stronger and more patient than such and such!' In 'al-Musnad' (1/305) via another chain which is narrated on the authority of Abu Tufail from Ibn 'Abbas that when the Messenger of Allaah -sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallamstopped at a place not far from Makkah called 'Marra az-Zahran' on his way to Umrah, it reached the Companions of the Messenger of Allaah -sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallamthat the Quraish were saying: What has caused them to come here feeble and thin? So his Companions said: 'If we slaughter our camels, eat from their meat and make a broth from its soup, then we will be relaxed when we enter upon the people tomorrow.' The Messenger said: 'Do not do that, but gather your provisions and bring them to me.' So they gathered them for the Messenger -sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallamand spread out a tablecloth made from leather. They ate, and had eaten satisfactorily, then left and every one of them took some in their sacks. Then the Messenger of Allaah -sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallamturned and went ahead until he entered the Masjid – and the Quraish were sitting near the black stone. The Messenger -sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallamuncovered one of his shoulders through his top garment then said: 'Do not let the people see you as deficient/weak.' S a l a f i P u b l i c a t i o n s . c o m Page 11 He touched the corner and entered Tawwaaf, passing by the Yemeni corner and walking to the Black Stone. The Quraish said: 'Just walking in the Tawwaaf does not please them, but they leap and jump like gazelles.' The Messenger -sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallamdid that for three circuits and so it became the Sunnah. Abu Tufail said: 'Ibn 'Abbas informed me that the Prophet -sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallamdid this in his farewell Hajj.' Its chain is also authentic according to the conditions of Imam Muslim. It was also narrated by al-Bayhaqi in his book 'Dala'il an-Naboowa' (3/3/1) through two routes. Muslim narrated it (4/64) by way of al-Jareeri on the authority of Abu Tufail with a similar narration and it has been researched in 'al-Irwaa' (4/315). It was also narrated via 'Atta on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: 'Indeed the Messenger of Allaah -sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallamwalked, and he walked briskly, around the House (Ka'bah) to show the Mushrikeen his strength.' Benefit: Perhaps someone might say: 'If the Shariah' reason for the brisk walk during the Tawwaaf was to show the Mushrikeen the strength of the Muslims, then can it not be said that as the reason for doing the action has ceased, then the legislation of the brisk walk should cease too?' The answer: No, because the Prophet - sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallam – walked briskly in his Tawwaaf after that first Umrah, in his farewell Hajj, as is mentioned in the long hadith of Jabir and by other than him, such as this hadith of Ibn Abbas with the previous narration of Abu Tufail. This is why Ibn Hibban said in his book 'sahih' (6/47 al-Ihsaan): 'So this objection has been cleared up and the brisk walk during Tawwaaf remains prescribed upon the Ummah of alMustafa - sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallam – until the Day of Judgement.' S a l a f i P u b l i c a t i o n s . c o m Page 12 Iltizaam No.2138On the authority of Abdullaah bin 'Amr who said the Messenger - sallAllaahu alayhi wa sallam used to place his chest, face, forearms and hands between the Black Stone and the door of the Ka'bah in his Tawwaaf.' S a l a f i P u b l i c a t i o n s . c o m Page 13 Arafat No.
content_id:
Sungguh hati ini bersaksi bahwa Nabi ﷺ tidak pernah mengucapkan satu huruf pun dari pernyataan ini. Semoga Allah memburukkan orang yang memalsukan ini.

Ramal. Alasan Syariat di Balik Langkah Cepat Saat Tawaf. No. 2573 — Dari Ibnu Abbas radhiyallahu 'anhu: 'Kaum Quraisy berkata: "Sungguh Muhammad dan para sahabatnya telah dilemahkan oleh demam Yatsrib." Maka pada tahun Rasulullah ﷺ melakukan umrah, beliau bersabda kepada para sahabatnya: "Berjalanlah dengan cepat mengelilingi Ka'bah agar kaum musyrikin dapat melihat kekuatan kalian." Maka ketika mereka berjalan dengan cepat, kaum Quraisy berkata: "Demam itu tidak melemahkan mereka."' Shahih. Silsilah Shahihah.

Syaikh Albani berkata: 'Sanad ini sahih sesuai syarat Imam Muslim. Bukhari meriwayatkannya tanpa sanad lengkap dalam Shahihnya (5/86) dari Hammad bin Salamah dengan riwayat serupa, begitu pula Hammad bin Zaid dari Ayyub dengan redaksi: "Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan mereka untuk berjalan cepat tiga putaran dan berjalan biasa di antara dua sudut Ka'bah agar kaum musyrikin dapat melihat kekuatan dan kesabaran mereka." Maka kaum musyrikin berkata: "Inilah orang-orang yang kalian klaim telah dilemahkan oleh demam. Mereka lebih kuat dan lebih sabar dari itu!" Dalam Al-Musnad (1/305) melalui sanad lain yang diriwayatkan dari Abu Thufail, dari Ibnu Abbas, bahwa ketika Rasulullah ﷺ singgah di suatu tempat tidak jauh dari Makkah yang disebut Marra Az-Zahran dalam perjalanannya menuju umrah, sampailah kepada para sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ bahwa kaum Quraisy berkata: Apa yang membuat mereka datang ke sini dalam keadaan lemah dan kurus? Maka para sahabatnya berkata: "Jika kita menyembelih unta kita, memakan dagingnya dan membuat kaldu dari kuahnya, maka kita akan lebih segar ketika menemui orang-orang esok hari." Rasul bersabda: "Jangan lakukan itu, tetapi kumpulkan perbekalan kalian dan bawa kepadaku." Maka mereka mengumpulkannya untuk Rasulullah ﷺ dan menggelar selembar alas kulit. Mereka makan hingga kenyang, lalu pergi dan masing-masing mengambil sebagian dalam kantong mereka. Kemudian Rasulullah ﷺ berpaling dan melanjutkan perjalanan hingga memasuki masjid — sementara kaum Quraisy duduk dekat Hajar Aswad. Rasulullah ﷺ membuka salah satu bahunya dari selendangnya kemudian bersabda: "Jangan biarkan orang-orang melihat kalian sebagai orang yang lemah."

Beliau menyentuh sudut Ka'bah dan memulai tawaf, melewati Rukun Yamani dan berjalan menuju Hajar Aswad. Kaum Quraisy berkata: "Sekadar berjalan dalam tawaf tidak memuaskan mereka, tetapi mereka melompat dan berlari seperti kijang." Rasulullah ﷺ melakukan itu selama tiga putaran dan kemudian menjadi Sunnah. Abu Thufail berkata: "Ibnu Abbas memberitahuku bahwa Nabi ﷺ melakukan ini dalam haji wada'nya." Sanadnya juga sahih sesuai syarat Imam Muslim. Al-Baihaqi juga meriwayatkannya dalam kitabnya Dala'il An-Nubuwwah (3/3/1) melalui dua jalur. Muslim meriwayatkannya (4/64) melalui Al-Jariri dari Abu Thufail dengan riwayat serupa dan telah diteliti dalam Al-Irwa' (4/315). Juga diriwayatkan melalui Atha' dari Ibnu Abbas yang berkata: "Sesungguhnya Rasulullah ﷺ berjalan, dan berjalan cepat, mengelilingi Baitullah (Ka'bah) untuk menunjukkan kekuatannya kepada kaum musyrikin."

Faedah: Mungkin seseorang akan berkata: "Jika alasan syariat di balik langkah cepat saat tawaf adalah untuk menunjukkan kekuatan umat Islam kepada kaum musyrikin, maka bukankah dapat dikatakan bahwa karena alasan melakukan tindakan tersebut telah hilang, maka pensyariatan langkah cepat pun harus berhenti?" Jawabannya: Tidak, karena Nabi ﷺ berjalan cepat dalam tawafnya setelah umrah pertama itu, dalam haji wada'nya, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam hadis panjang Jabir dan selainnya, seperti hadis Ibnu Abbas ini dengan riwayat Abu Thufail sebelumnya. Inilah mengapa Ibnu Hibban berkata dalam kitabnya Shahih (6/47 Al-Ihsan): "Maka keberatan ini telah dijawab dan langkah cepat saat tawaf tetap disyariatkan bagi umat Al-Mustafa ﷺ hingga Hari Kiamat."

Iltizam. No. 2138 — Dari Abdullah bin Amr yang berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ biasa meletakkan dada, wajah, lengan bawah, dan tangannya di antara Hajar Aswad dan pintu Ka'bah dalam tawafnya.

Arafah.
